item.title: Hundreds of jeepney drivers in Manila still left off fuel subsidy list
item.description: MANILA — More than 350 jeepney drivers in Manila were still left off the list of recipients for the fuel subsidy as the DSWD and LTFRB conducted special payouts for PUJ drivers in the city on Wednesday, April 15.

item.title: Ordinance violations drop as public compliance improves under Safer Cities drive — PNP
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) reported a continued decline in daily ordinance violations across Metro Manila, saying the trend suggests that more residents are becoming familiar with local rules and choosing to comply.

item.title: P49-M worth of alleged illicit cigarettes seized in Negros; 2 arrested
item.description: Police seized more than P49 million worth of suspected illicit cigarettes and arrested two individuals in a series of operations in Negros Occidental from April 13 to 14, the Police Regional Office–Negros Island Region (PRO-NIR) said.

item.title: Gov't launches programs for OFWs displaced by Middle East war
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Wednesday ordered government agencies to prioritize and ensure economic security of over 5,000 overseas Filipino workers and their dependents who had been repatriated from the Middle East due to US-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: Blue Ribbon suspends flood control hearings
item.description: The Senate Blue Ribbon Committee said Wednesday it was suspending its hearings on the flood control scandal until there were enough senators to advance its partial committee report to the plenary session.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
